programmes.show_description: The Cage. The team are called by a petrified owner to investigate a St Osyth building that she believes is haunted by the witches that it used to hold. S1, Ep12
Corn Exchange. In Hertford, the team investigate reports of strange sounds and sensations in a music venue. A former jail, are the shadows of the past affecting the present? S2, Ep1
Stable Cottage Buntingford. The team are called in to investigate sinister activity at a grade 2 listed cottage. The residents are increasingly disturbed by unexplainable events. S2, Ep2
Teleshopping.
Drew decides that he needs something different to stand out from the competition. He and Tee head to Italy hoping to find some antiques that his rivals won't be able to match. S13, Ep1
Sensing the chance to get some bargains, Drew checks in on some contacts who are downsizing and selling off collections. In Norwich, he finds a beautiful Victorian umbrella stand. S13, Ep12
Gaby Roslin helps Loose Women's Sherrie Hewson to find a holiday home in Spain. She has a budget of up to £500,000 for a stunning property. S1, Ep5

Prospective buyers around the UK wanting to swap hectic city living for a home in the country are shown an array of beautiful rural properties. S17, Ep24
Drew and Tee head get an exclusive look at a private collection of rock 'n roll memorabilia. Drew is excited to find some very rare Led Zeppelin and Motorhead collectibles. S15, Ep11
Drew and Tee head to a family run antiques business in Yorkshire with a no-nonsense owner. He has to haggle hard, but Drew bags himself an elegant 19th century bachelor's chest. S15, Ep12
Drew and Tee seek out the very best antiques the Isle of Wight has to offer. Their first stop is the seaside town of Ventnor, a fashionable holiday resort in Victorian times. S15, Ep13
Charles Hanson and Christina Trevanion begin their search for hidden gems in Morecambe. One of the experts spends all of their budget on four items. S19, Ep1
Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson continue their road trip in Frodsham. Charles spends big on a collection of spoons and a table croquet set. S19, Ep2
Wood restorer specialist Will restores a handmade childhood chair. Plus, bookbinder Chris revives a treasured wedding album that was damaged in a house fire. S6, Ep22
Radio Gaga. A vast collection of gramophones and vintage radios get the auction buzzing. Plus, a 15-carat gold charm bracelet and a rare royal mug steal the show. S1, Ep9/10

programmes.show_name: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programmes.show_description: A man left on a stranger's doorstep uncovers a painful family secret. A woman learns why she was left in the back of an Aston Martin car in the 1950s. S5, Ep2

programmes.show_name: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programmes.show_description: A police detective left on church steps discovers the truth about his own identity. A woman left in a hospital in the 1960s gets surprising news. S5, Ep3
Action Stations! A battalion of Action Man toys, an old RAF jacket, and a Second World War sweetheart brooch end up in a bidding war as they go under Angus's gavel. S5, Ep11
Seaside To Stateside. Angus tries to fund a couple's move to the US by selling their precious gemstones and retro video games, and later unearths some pricey 70s furniture. S5, Ep12
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep11
A recently retired woman wants to declutter her home and raise money to visit her family abroad. Angus travels to Horsforth to dig through her collection. S2, Ep3
Angus and the team are in Harrogate to look through an impressive Art Deco collection. Later, they hunt for hidden gems at a cottage in Shropshire. S2, Ep4
Little Easton Manor. The team investigate a picturesque mansion in a small village in Essex. Can they find the cause of the reported strange sounds and apparitions? S2, Ep3
Smugglers Inn. In Sussex, the team investigate a historic inn with a gruesome past. The new owners ignored the dark reputation, but strange phenomena are giving them second thoughts. S2, Ep4
Uncle Tom's Cabin. The team of paranormal experts visit an inn in Wincanton, Somerset, and witness some unusual activity. Plus, Jane finds herself at the mercy of a possible spirit. S2, Ep5

Drew and Tee head to a grade II listed mansion in Lincolnshire. The house is packed with items and Drew haggles for a superb 19th century chair and a decorated chinoiserie mirror. S15, Ep14
The boys stop at a salvage yard which Drew hails as the best in the country. They find a pair of beautifully crafted sundials and an overthrow believed to be from Belvoir Castle. S15, Ep15
Cotswolds. Drew finally opens his own antiques shop in his home town of Conwy. So, he goes to the Cotswolds and one of the UK's largest indoor antiques, looking for treasure. S12, Ep1

Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson hunt for interesting items in Kirkby Malzeard. Who will make the most profit at the auction in Rotherham? S19, Ep4
Soft toy restorers Julie and Amanda revive a threadbare toy rabbit. Drum expert Pete restores a battered bass drum and furniture restorer Will works on a wooden church pew. S6, Ep23
